title: orthosiphon virgatus
intro: Orthosiphon virgatus, also known as Asian pennywort or Misai Kuching, is a type of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. The health benefits of Orthosiphon virgatus include reducing blood pressure, improving kidney function, and alleviating symptoms of urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
